title: daucus heterocarpus
intro: Daucus heterocarpus, also known as wild carrot or Queen Anne's lace, is a plant species that belongs to the parsley family (Apiaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Daucus heterocarpus include reducing fever, relieving menstrual cramps, and treating digestive issues. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
